tag.name: Consent Mode | Default Consent Mode
tag.notes:
The default state should always be set up with the assumption that OneTrust will fail to load. If it was to fail to load or is slow to load, what would we want the default behavior to be? 

We leverage the regions option here to essentially mirror your default OneTrust settings. For example, if the defaults should be Opt-In for in EMEA and Opt-Out for Florida and split for California (yes for performance but no for targeting), then set that up here. You can read more about region options here: https://developers.google.com/tag-platform/devguides/consent#region-specific_behavior. If legal and the security team are ok with using a single global setting, then we can remove the conditionals here and just set a global default.

The wait_for_update parameter enables us to wait a certain period of time in the hopes that OneTrust will complete its asynchronous load and run the JS we need to determine consent state from it before firing initial beacons. This helps us in situations where we fire page_view or other similar page-load based events (view_item on PDPs for example) so that we don't prevent firing of those due to OneTrust being slow.

trigger.name: Page load - Home Recruiting Landing Page
Page load - All Pages - DOM Ready
Page load - DL - Contact Us Thanks Push

Event - Request More Info Thank you

Page load - DL - Request More Info Push
